mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS both rise, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns specifically during recovery regimes, or the mechanism reverses direction when supply constraints dominate.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust consumer spending capacity and corporate hiring, which directly supports earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.5%, markets price in delayed monetary policy response and margin compression, resulting in negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5% threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment signal synchronized strength in the construction sector and labor market, driving positive equity returns through improved economic sentiment.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative or flat returns when HOUST rises and UNRATE falls, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% during late-cycle expansions, corporate earnings revisions turn negative and risk premia expand, causing sustained negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth drops below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to predict downturns during late-cycle phases.
